subject: Re: Hash
body:
give him clothes and guitar

--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

> Would it be ok for Walter Novembre to give Hash's
> brother, Ash, Hash's  
> clothes and guitar?  Seems unlikely that the x-wife
> would  complain.   Walter 
> claims that Ash is getting fairly angry and Ash 
> wants to call you to tell you off. 
>  
>  
> On the other hand, I don't want the wife or the
> brother later claiming that  
> we were holding onto Hash's property (e.g., money
> that is owed, Hash's 
> computer,  etc.) unlawfully.  We are currently
> taking the position that we cannot 
> give  the property to anyone without legal authority
> evidenced by letters 
> testamentary  or similar evidence of authority.  By
> giving the clothes and the guitar 
>  without such evidence, do we undermine that
> position somehow?  Walter's  
> said to me that we should just say that the house
> manager turned over the stuff  
> to Ash without authority, but you know how I feel
> about that approach to  
> things.
>  
> As a practical matter, I can't imagine that giving
> the clothes and guitar  to 
> Ash would really create such problems, but in an
> overabundance of  caution . 
> . . 
>  
> Walter had to take a call from LHW before I could
> speak further with Walter  
> about this, but I figured I'd get your views before
> speaking with Walter  
> again.
>  
> Please advise.
>

body_html:
give him clothes and guitar

--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

Would it be ok for Walter Novembre to give Hash's
brother, Ash, Hash's
clothes and guitar? Seems unlikely that the x-wife
would complain. Walter
claims that Ash is getting fairly angry and Ash
wants to call you to tell you off.

On the other hand, I don't want the wife or the
brother later claiming that
we were holding onto Hash's property (e.g., money
that is owed, Hash's
computer, etc.) unlawfully. We are currently
taking the position that we cannot
give the property to anyone without legal authority
evidenced by letters
testamentary or similar evidence of authority. By
giving the clothes and the guitar
without such evidence, do we undermine that
position somehow? Walter's
said to me that we should just say that the house
manager turned over the stuff
to Ash without authority, but you know how I feel
about that approach to
things.

As a practical matter, I can't imagine that giving
the clothes and guitar to
Ash would really create such problems, but in an
overabundance of caution .
. .

Walter had to take a call from LHW before I could
speak further with Walter
about this, but I figured I'd get your views before
speaking with Walter
again.

Please advise.

Darren K. Indyke
Attorney-At-Law
457 Madison Avenue
4th Floor
New York, New York 10022
